data.title: Paychex outlines 12% Q4 revenue growth target as Paycor integration and AI drive momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Paychex (PAYX) Q3 2026

Management View

  • John Gibson, President and CEO, reported a strong quarter with revenue up 20% and adjusted operating income up 22% year-over-year, highlighting the "effective execution and progress advancing our strategic priorities, most notably, the Paycor integration and acceleration of our transformational AI initiatives."
  • Gibson emphasized robust free cash flow generation and the company’s competitive advantage in compliance and advisory expertise, noting, "Our compliance depth, advisory expertise and award-winning platforms provide a clear competitive advantage in navigating a constantly changing and complex regulatory environment."
  • Gibson explained that AI implementation is strengthening the company’s advantage, with over 500 AI-powered capabilities enhancing productivity. "We now have over 500 AI-powered capabilities and agents that can drive higher productivity and smarter decisions and outcomes," said Gibson.
  • Gibson also provided updates on the Paycor integration, stating, "We remain on track to exceed our fiscal '26 synergy targets we discussed last quarter. Leading indicators such as bookings and broker referrals have reaccelerated to pre-acquisition levels, and we are adding sales headcount to capture the demand we see."
  • The company reported strong growth in retirement, ASO, and PEO, with PEO worksite employee growth in the high single digits and record retention rates. Gibson said, "Our PEO business remains strong with high single-digit worksite employee growth, driven by robust demand and record retention rates."
  • Robert Schrader, CFO, stated, "Total revenue increased 20% over the prior year to $1.8 billion. This represents an acceleration in the organic growth of the business relative to the first half of the year."
  • Schrader added, "Operating income margin was 43.8%, and adjusted operating income margins increased approximately 80 basis points to 47.7%, driven by increased productivity and cost discipline while increasing our investments in AI."
  • Schrader shared, "Diluted earnings per share increased 9% to $1.56 per share and adjusted diluted earnings per share increased 15% to $1.71 per share."

Outlook

  • Schrader reaffirmed the fiscal '26 outlook except for an upward revision in interest on funds held for clients, now expected to be in the range of $200 million to $210 million.
  • Fourth quarter growth is anticipated to be approximately 12% with an adjusted operating margin of 41% to 42%.
  • Schrader indicated, "We believe Paychex has never been better positioned to succeed in the AI era of HCM and deliver shareholder value."

Financial Results

  • Management Solutions revenue grew 23% to $1.4 billion, driven by product penetration and price realization; Paycor contributed approximately 19 percentage points to growth.
  • PEO and Insurance Solutions revenue increased 9% to $398 million, primarily due to growth in average PEO worksite employees and increased PEO insurance revenues.
  • Interest on funds held for clients increased 33% to $57 million, largely due to the addition of Paycor balances.
  • Total expenses grew 24% to just over $1 billion, largely attributed to the Paycor acquisition, with expenses excluding Paycor estimated to be up in the low single digits.
  • Operating cash flows were nearly $2 billion year-to-date; free cash flows increased 27% year-over-year.
  • The company returned $463 million in the quarter and over $1.5 billion year-to-date to shareholders via dividends and share buybacks.

Q&A

  • Bryan Bergin, TD Cowen, asked about organic growth and Q4 outlook. Gibson responded that "the organic growth of the business has been a bit weaker" but has shown "sequential improvement each quarter," with the back half organic growth rate "closer to 6%."
  • Mark Marcon, Baird, inquired about sales and competitive environment. Gibson noted, "We were accelerating par and bookings growth in the third quarter," and highlighted momentum in enterprise and PEO segments.
  • Marcon also questioned margin expansion and AI’s role. Gibson stated, "If we grow the top line, we are going to be able to grow margins and expand margins over time...we are innovators in that regard and are going to grab every tool we can."
  • Tien-Tsin Huang, JPMorgan, asked about the advisory business and AI's impact. Gibson clarified, "There's no way that I think technology is going to replace that, at least that I see in the short term."
  • Bryan Keane, Citi, sought details on PEO insurance growth. Schrader attributed it to "strength in the underlying operating performance" and January enrollment, while Gibson emphasized success in larger deals and upmarket expansion.
  • Further questions from analysts covered revenue timing, synergy realization, pricing, AI monetization, headcount expansion, and capital allocation, with management providing detailed responses and affirming confidence in guidance.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on organic growth, margin expansion, and synergy realization, with a neutral to slightly positive tone, often seeking specifics on strategic levers and future growth.
  • Management maintained a confident and proactive tone in prepared remarks and during Q&A, frequently referencing sequential improvements, progress in integration, and AI-driven productivity. Gibson used phrases like "I feel good about where we are" and "we are innovators in that regard."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the overall sentiment from both analysts and management shifted slightly more positive, reflecting improved organic growth and successful integration milestones.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from a low-end bias in the prior quarter to reaffirmed and slightly increased interest income outlook in the current quarter.
  • Strategic focus has moved to cross-selling opportunities, accelerating AI deployment, and leveraging the Paycor acquisition for upmarket growth.
  • Management’s tone has grown more confident, highlighting robust progress in integration and organic growth acceleration, while analysts appear more constructive on near-term growth and margin prospects.
  • Key metrics such as revenue growth, operating margin, and free cash flow have all shown sequential improvements, and cost discipline remains a priority.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted potential timing variances in revenue recognition, particularly in PEO and insurance.
  • Market uncertainty and macroeconomic factors continue to pose risks, with management emphasizing prudence in guidance and planning.
  • Analysts raised questions on pricing power, competitive environment, and sustainability of growth, which management addressed by pointing to strong retention, diversified client base, and continued investment in technology and sales headcount.

Final Takeaway

Management underscored that Paychex is entering fiscal year '27 with strong momentum, a solid balance sheet, and enhanced capabilities following the successful Paycor integration and rapid AI adoption. The company believes its differentiated advisory model, expanded product offerings, and disciplined execution position it to deliver sustained growth, margin expansion, and shareholder value in a dynamic and highly regulated HCM market.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Albemarle starts environmental review for $3.1B Chile lithium project
data.description:
Albemarle (ALB) up 3.2% in Wednesday's trading after saying it launched the environmental review process in Chile for its first project using direct lithium extraction technology.

The world's largest publicly traded lithium producer said the project is designed to ​recover nearly twice as much lithium while reducing the amount ​of brine extracted compared to current operations.

The full ⁠project would consist of a DLE plant ‌within ⁠Albemarle's (ALB) mining concession area, with up to six processing trains at the heart of Chile's salt flats, and the construction of a power transmission line, requiring a ~$3.1B total investment and a useful life extending through 2045, the company said in ​a filing to the Chilean environmental assessment agency.

Net brine extraction would ​drop from 442 to ​342 liters ⁠per second with one DLE train in operation, and as low as 142 liters per second with ​all six trains in use, the company said in the filing.

Native communities around Chile's vast Atacama salt flat have raised concerns ⁠that ​mining could deplte scarce reserves of freshwater ​and lithium-rich brine, reducing its availability for people and wildlife.

data.title: Generac shares drop as investor day lacks hyperscaler deal
data.description:
Generac Holdings (GNRC) shares fell as much as 12% on Wednesday before paring losses, as investors reacted to the company’s investor day presentation that highlighted strong demand trends but stopped short of delivering a key anticipated announcement.

The selloff came even as Generac (GNRC) pointed to accelerating momentum in its data center business, a segment that has become central to its long-term growth narrative. The company said its backlog for data center-related products has surged roughly 75% over the past six weeks, underscoring rising demand tied to artificial intelligence infrastructure and cloud computing buildouts.

However, investor expectations appeared to be set even higher. Shares weakened after the company didn’t unveil an anticipated long-term agreement with a hyperscale data center customer, a development many had been looking for as validation of Generac’s (GNRC) positioning in the space.

Management used the presentation to reinforce its role in what it described as a “generational” shift in power demand. Slides highlighted how electricity consumption is expected to accelerate sharply, driven in large part by data centers, which the company said could account for more than half of load growth by 2030.

Generac (GNRC) also emphasized that backup power is becoming increasingly critical infrastructure for data centers, where uptime requirements are stringent and outages can lead to significant financial and operational damage.

Despite the market’s lukewarm reaction, the company maintained its financial outlook. Generac (GNRC) reaffirmed its 2026 guidance, calling for revenue growth in the mid-teens, with residential sales expected to rise around 10% and commercial and industrial revenue projected to grow in the low- to mid-20% range. Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization margins are expected to remain in the 18% to 19% range.

The broader backdrop supporting Generac’s (GNRC) strategy was also a focal point. The company pointed to a strained electrical grid, rising power prices and accelerating load growth as structural tailwinds that could drive long-term demand for its products.

Still, Wednesday’s market reaction suggested that investors are looking for more tangible proof points, particularly large-scale customer agreements, to justify the stock’s positioning around the data center theme.

data.title: Realty Income rises after six sessions of decline
data.description:
Shares of Realty Income (O) rose 0.07% to $60.50 in the afternoon trade on Wednesday, snapping a six-session losing streak.

The stock has fallen around 5.7% between March 17 and March 24 compared to a 2.38% decline in the S&P 500 during the same period.

The decline came amid persistent pressure on the housing sector, with U.S. homebuilder sentiment remaining below the 50 breakeven level for 23 straight months as builders grapple with elevated construction and labor costs, while higher mortgage rates driven by rising U.S. Treasury yields linked to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran have kept buyers on the sidelines.

Separately, housing analysts expect home prices to rise only modestly as borrowing costs stay elevated near 6%, with affordability constraints, supply shortages, and a lack of near-term policy relief weighing on demand and limiting prospects for a meaningful market recovery.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, O is rated with a Strong Buy, with a score of 4.63 out of 5, receiving an A both in terms of growth and profitability, but a C+ in terms of valuation.

A recent Seeking Alpha analysis said, Realty Income is likely to see tempered growth amid rising rates, noting that there are “large odds for elevated interest rates in the near future,” which could increase financing costs for its acquisition-driven model. The analyst added that valuation already reflects these pressures, pointing out that “the current valuation multiple already priced in the headwinds from higher bond rates.”

While on Wall Street, seven of 24 analysts rate the stock a buy or higher, 16 recommend a hold, and one of them rates it with a sell or lower.

Shares have fallen around 8.3% in the past month but remain up about 7% year-to-date.

data.title: Costco creates some buzz in the energy drink market
data.description:
Costco Wholesale (COST) has quietly launched a new Kirkland energy drink at some stores. While the company has not issued a formal announcement with details about the launch, social media accounts have posted pictures of 24-pack Kirkland Signature Sparkling Energy Drink products being sold in three flavor varieties. Early reports suggest the product is selling well during its first week on the shelves.

The drink is a Kirkland house-brand sparkling energy drink in 12-ounce cans, with 200 mg of caffeine, zero sugar, and about 10 calories per can. Reported flavors include Tropical, Peach, and Orange, and the package price is $16.99 for 24 cans, or about $0.70 each.

Notably, Google search interest for the query "Costco energy drinks" spiked this week.

 

 

 

The launch is interesting because Costco (COST) does sell Red Bull, Monster (MNST), and Celsius (CELH) energy drinks at many locations.

Weighing in on the development, TD Cowen analyst Robert Moskow said the sell-off this week in Celsius Holdings (CELH) looks like an overreaction given that Costco only represents ~10% of sales and the category has not historically had strong private label entrants. He reminded investors that 7-Eleven's private label energy drink launch in 2024 did not have meaningful implications for branded energy drink market share, even as the largest convenience store in the U.S. "We view the pullback as a buying opportunity given strong distribution tailwinds in 2026," wrote Moskow on the setup for Celsius (CELH).

data.title: HP continues AI device push but fails to provide meaningful catalyst: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
HP's (HPQ) Imagine event continued management's focus to develop AI devices for hybrid work, but the new hardware failed to move the needle for a notable inflection in either direction as the PC market deteriorates, according to Morgan Stanley.

The Palo Alto-based company introduced a new lineup of high-powered workstations, AI PCs, and laptops. They also revealed what they called the "future of work" through HP IQ, a connected ecosystem of intelligent devices designed to make work more seamless across devices, spaces, and experiences through a new workplace layer.

HP shares had increased nearly 4% by afternoon trading on Wednesday.

"As a result, our near-to-medium-term outlook doesn't change," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Erik Woodring, in an investor note. "And as we look out over the next 12 months, we believe the challenges that HPQ and the broader PC industry faces are building, with memory price inflation and shortages intensifying, CPU shortages and price hikes also escalating, and deteriorating 1H notebook ODM builds indicating a weakening of the PC market faster than we expected just 3 months ago, all contributing to our new PC market forecast that assumes 16% Y/Y global shipment declines vs. expectations for flat to -5% Y/Y declines just 3 months ago."

Morgan Stanley maintained its Underweight rating and $16 price target on HP.

data.title: Microsoft 365, Copilot narratives need to improve for stock to move higher: UBS
data.description:
The narrative surrounding Microsoft's (MSFT) 365 and Copilot offerings needs to improve for the tech giant's stock to move higher, investment firm UBS said, following recent meetings with investors.

“At 19x CY26E non-GAAP EPS we remain Buy-rated, but acknowledge that the narrative around M365/Copilot needs to improve in order for the stock to really re-rate higher,” analyst Karl Keirstead wrote in a note to clients. As such, Keirstead lowered his price target to $510 from $600.

The recent efforts from OpenAI (OPENAI) and Anthropic (ANTHRO) to move into knowledge work hits at Microsoft's “sweet spot,” but investor focus has shifted to how durable and what kind of growth can come from 365 and Copilot, Keirstead explained.

Upon meeting with Microsoft's investor relations team, Keirstead said the Windows giant pointed to usage trends and seat trends picking up in the fiscal second quarter. And notably, recent moves, such as Copilot Cowork, a new E7 bundle, and switching up the leadership, “signal a more aggressive stance and sense of urgency inside Microsoft,” Keirstead added.

Aside from 365 and Copilot, Microsoft “sounded very bullish about Azure demand” but did not offer updates on revenue growth and capacity additions beyond what it has already told investors, Keirstead explained.

data.title: The U.S. and Iran are headed towards military escalation despite market optimism – Rapidan Energy Group CEO
data.description:
Scott Modell, CEO of energy and geopolitical analysis firm Rapidan Energy Group, warned that the U.S. and Iran are headed toward military escalation despite market optimism about a potential ceasefire.

In an interview with CNBC, Modell argued that oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) should be higher given the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and that the physical realities of supply shortages will soon override the current narrative of de-escalation.

The analyst dismissed the reported 15-point peace plan sent by the U.S. to Iran, noting that both nations hold “very, very extremist positions” that remain “entrenched.”

Modell pointed out there are no indications of substantive talks occurring, stating, “There’s no way that President Trump can back out of this without opening the strait. So opening talks is a nonstarter for the Iranians at this point.”

While oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) have trended lower, Modell credited this to what he called a “master class in verbal intervention by the president” aimed at keeping markets calm.

However, he cautioned that this approach has limits, predicting that “physical realities of markets, shortages, lack of tankers, fighting over barrels is going to take over.”

Modell highlighted that the U.S. has amassed its largest military concentration in the Middle East since the 2003 invasion of Iraq, with thousands of troops now in theater. He suggested military action could begin as early as Friday, with potential moves to seize islands and initiate suppressive fire against Iranian positions disrupting tanker traffic.

Iran has demanded five key points in exchange for ending the war while treating the Strait of Hormuz as what Modell described as their “own tollbooth”—a position he characterized as a “nonstarter” for the U.S. administration. The closure of the critical shipping lane has created an inflection point where military action appears to be the only remaining option.

The analyst predicted that market sentiment would shift by early April as military escalation forces investors to confront reality.

“I think it’s just a matter of time when military escalation in the Gulf is going to force people to say, ‘Wow.’ We are in something that actually looks like it is a militarily necessary reopening of the strait,” Modell said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

More on the Middle East conflict

  • Iran’s five conditions to end the war in the Middle East – report
  • Iran does not accept a ceasefire – report
  • U.S. reportedly has a plan to end the Iran war
  • Trump: Iran offered the U.S. a ‘present’ to show good faith in war resolution negotiations
  • Odds of U.S.-Iran nuclear deal rise after Trump says agreement made

data.title: FuboTV stands as the best performing large-cap communication services stock YTD
data.description:
U.S. large-cap communication services stocks have fell in 2026, with the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLC) down about 5.2% year-to-date through March.

In light of this, below is a list of the top 10 large-cap communication services stocks with market capitalizations of $10B or more, ranked by their year-to-date performance.

The list is topped by FuboTV (FUBO), with a commanding YTD performance of 376.98%, far outpacing the rest of the field. The New York Times Company (NYT) and AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (ASTS) are the primary runners-up, with YTD gains of 21.23% and 19.76%, respectively.

The top 10 features a diverse mix of companies across the communication services sector. Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. (LYV) represents the movies and entertainment industry, while Charter Communications, Inc. (CHTR) and EchoStar Corporation (SATS) come from the cable and satellite space. The bottom half of the list includes several names with negative YTD performance, including Electronic Arts Inc. (EA), Comcast Corporation (CMCSA), Warner Bros. Discovery, Inc. (WBD), and Omnicom Group Inc. (OMC).

Here is the list:

  1. FuboTV Inc. (FUBO), YTD perf: 376.98%, Quant rating: Sell 1.50

  2. The New York Times Company (NYT), YTD perf: 21.23%, Quant rating: Hold 3.26

  3. AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (ASTS), YTD perf: 19.76%, Quant rating: Hold 3.30

  4. Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. (LYV), YTD perf: 7.16%, Quant rating: Hold 2.85

  5. Charter Communications, Inc. (CHTR), YTD perf: 4.24%, Quant rating: Buy 3.99

  6. EchoStar Corporation (SATS), YTD perf: 1.97%, Quant rating: Hold 3.44

  7. Electronic Arts Inc. (EA), YTD perf: -1.33%, Quant rating: Hold 3.05

  8. Comcast Corporation (CMCSA), YTD perf: -2.24%, Quant rating: Hold 3.30

  9. Warner Bros. Discovery, Inc. (WBD), YTD perf: -5.34%, Quant rating: Hold 3.39

  10. Omnicom Group Inc. (OMC), YTD perf: -6.64%, Quant rating: Strong Buy 4.58

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL)
